Monella collina, also known as Bee Balm, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive flowers and fragrance. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and protect against oxidative damage. Monarda contains a range of bioactive constituents, including rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and monoterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Monella collina include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as topical creams and salves for skin and wound care.
